title: Is the Grinch Stealing This Year's Holiday Season Jobs?
text: United Parcel Service Inc. NYSE: UPS is among logistics companies that are proceeding cautiously this year, in anticipation of a slower holiday season.

title: 3 Stocks to Sell Ahead of the Coming VIX Spike
text: Uncertainty is something that we can evaluate in the financial markets in general. There are many tools and indicators that we can monitor to visualize and quantify uncertainty at a general level in the markets, but there is something that is completely individual and that is risk aversion.

title: 3 Dividend-Paying Transportation Stocks to Watch Currently
text: Transportation stocks like FedEx (FDX), Expeditors (EXPD) and Delta (DAL) are well-served by their dividend-paying capacity.

title: 3 Transport-Service Stocks to Watch Despite Industry Concerns
text: The Zacks Industry Rank for the Transportation-Services industry paints a dull picture mainly due to the freight demand slowdown. We believe stocks like EXPD, CHRW and MATX are better positioned to escape industry challenges.

title: Here's Why Investors Should Retain Expeditors (EXPD) Now
text: Investor-friendly steps and solid liquidity boost Expeditors (EXPD).

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio - Closing In On A Historic First Year
text: The 4-factor dividend growth portfolio is a strategy that leverages the stock selection process of Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF, with a few minor twists. The portfolio fell by 0.57% in August, outperforming the S&P 500 by 1.02%. Year-to-date, the portfolio is up 14.07%. Since inception, the portfolio is generating 3.95% of alpha over the S&P 500.

title: Expeditors International: Dividend Trends By The Numbers
text: Expeditors International of Washington, Inc. is demonstrating interesting technicals that point to rising prices over time. The company has a history of aggressive, consistent dividend growth, which indicates fundamental strength. EXPD has a solid dividend payout ratio, low debt-to-equity ratio, and strong cash flow, suggesting continued dividend growth.
